headline: BT Group and Verizon to form joint venture, creating a scaled international connectivity platform for multinational customers

headline: Alphabet Stock Joins the Dow but It’s Rising for a Different Reason
summary: The shares were up 1.2% in premarket trading.  The Google-parent is replacing  Verizon Communications  in the 30-stock Dow index from Monday’s session, in a move announced last week.  Because the Dow is a price-weighted index—meaning stock price rather than market capitalization determine the weighting of each company—few funds choose to replicate its composition.

headline: Florida, Pennsylvania, Or California? Which Retiree Keeps The Most Money?
summary: Three retirees own the same portfolio. Each holds $1 million in dividend-paying stocks generating roughly $60,000 a year of income. One lives in Florida, one in Pennsylvania, and one in California. By the time the year ends, they will not have the same amount of spending power, even though their brokerage statements look identical. State ... Florida, Pennsylvania, Or California? Which Retiree Keeps The Most Money?

content.title: Florida, Pennsylvania, Or California? Which Retiree Keeps The Most Money?

headline: Comcast, Alphabet, Palantir, SpaceX, Microsoft, and More Stocks That Explain Today’s Market
summary: Technology stocks were trading higher in premarket trading Monday as the  looked set to finally snap its five-day losing streak.  Semiconductor companies, other artificial-intelligence stocks, and the Magnificent Seven were among those rebounding to start the week.  Microsoft  jumped 2% ahead of the open, while  Amazon Alphabet Nvidia Meta  and  Tesla  were all up around 1%.

headline: Victra, Verizon's Largest Exclusive Authorized Retailer, Recognized Among Comparably's 2026 Best Sales Teams
summary: Victra, the largest exclusive authorized Verizon retailer in the U.S., has been named a winner of Comparably's 2026 Best Sales Teams Award. The recognition is based entirely on anonymous employee feedback collected over the past 12 months and reflects the enthusiasm, engagement, and satisfaction of Victra's sales associates across its nationwide retail footprint.

headline: Comcast Plots Breakup to Take on ‘Big Three’ Telecoms
summary: In its latest structural shake-up,  Comcast  intends to split into two companies through a tax-free spinoff of NBCUniversal and Sky in roughly a year’s time.  Under the plan announced Monday, one company will house cable and technology operations while the other holds the NBCUniversal and Sky media businesses.  Comcast  expects to retain up to a 19.9% stake in the newly formed media entity for a year following the split.

content.title: Comcast Plots Breakup to Take on ‘Big Three’ Telecoms
content.summary: In its latest structural shake-up,  Comcast  intends to split into two companies through a tax-free spinoff of NBCUniversal and Sky in roughly a year’s time.  Under the plan announced Monday, one company will house cable and technology operations while the other holds the NBCUniversal and Sky media businesses.  Comcast  expects to retain up to a 19.9% stake in the newly formed media entity for a year following the split.

headline: Alphabet shares 'a good value play', analyst says
summary:
STORY: "A lot of people are kind of worried about their spending habits" regarding AI, Lang said, "but I think today is a good day. On June 29th, I think the stock is turning around here."

Alphabet shares climbed on Monday as the Google parent made its debut in the blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average, replacing Verizon Communications, and immediately ranking among its most influential members.

"And look, I mean, they came in with really strong earnings the last couple of quarters in April and in January," Lang noted. "And I don't think that that's going to reverse to the downside. And I think people are picking up Alphabet, Google here as a bargain right now coming into the next quarter, which they will report towards the end of July."

content.title: Alphabet shares 'a good value play', analyst says
content.summary:
STORY: "A lot of people are kind of worried about their spending habits" regarding AI, Lang said, "but I think today is a good day. On June 29th, I think the stock is turning around here."

Alphabet shares climbed on Monday as the Google parent made its debut in the blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average, replacing Verizon Communications, and immediately ranking among its most influential members.

"And look, I mean, they came in with really strong earnings the last couple of quarters in April and in January," Lang noted. "And I don't think that that's going to reverse to the downside. And I think people are picking up Alphabet, Google here as a bargain right now coming into the next quarter, which they will report towards the end of July."

headline: Dow Finally Tops 52,000 With Some Help From Its Newest Member
summary: The Dow finally closed above the 52,000 the same day its newest member joined a rally in Big Tech and chip stocks.  Alphabet, which replaced Verizon in the Dow this week, led the index.  “This month has been VERY volatile — some of the swings have been outright historic, and in the moment, honestly a bit unsettling,” writes Mizuho’s Daniel O’Regan.
